user.title: Set up Dragonfly
user.description: Dragonfly tracking needs no account or postal code — the hub is created right away. Add parcels afterwards via the integration options or the `dragonfly.track_parcel` service.

data_description.add: Enter a Dragonfly Track & Trace code to start tracking it. You find it on the shipping confirmation or the missed-delivery card.

data_description.delivered_filter_type: Show delivered parcels from the last X days, or limit to the X most recent.
data_description.delivered_filter_amount: Number of days or parcels to show.

data_description.include_history: Add a per-parcel history attribute listing each status update (timestamp, status, original text). Off by default.

data_description.refresh_interval: How often the integration checks Dragonfly for parcel updates. A slower interval is gentler on Dragonfly's API; the default of 30 minutes is plenty for most parcels.

error.invalid_tracking_code: That does not look like a Dragonfly tracking code.
error.already_tracked: That parcel is already being tracked.

track_parcel.name: Track parcel
track_parcel.description: Start tracking a Dragonfly parcel by its Track & Trace code.

untrack_parcel.name: Untrack parcel
untrack_parcel.description: Stop tracking a Dragonfly parcel by its Track & Trace code.

tracking_code.name: Tracking code
tracking_code.description: The Dragonfly Track & Trace code to stop tracking.

trigger_type.parcel_delivery_time_changed: A parcel's expected delivery time changed
